name: Role of HTML
text: What does HTML do in a webpage?
explanation: HTML is structure + content. CSS = style. JavaScript = interactivity.

name: Role of CSS
text: What does CSS do?
explanation: CSS = Cascading Style Sheets. It styles HTML's appearance.

name: DOCTYPE purpose
text: What does `<!DOCTYPE html>` do?
explanation: DOCTYPE tells the browser to render in HTML5 standards mode.

name: head vs body
text: Which tag contains visible content?
explanation: body = visible content. head = metadata (title, links, meta).

name: Alt attribute
text: Why add alt text to images?
options: To describe them for screen readers and when they fail to load
explanation: Alt text describes the image for people who can't see it + fallback if image fails.

name: Link new tab
text: Which attribute opens a link in a new tab?
explanation: target="_blank" is the standard way to open a link in a new tab/window.

name: Semantic h1
text: How many `<h1>` tags should a well-structured page have?
options: Exactly one — the main page heading
explanation: One h1 per page is the modern semantic convention.

name: ol vs ul
text: When should you use `<ol>`?
explanation: ol = ordered. Use when sequence is meaningful.

name: Meta charset
text: Why include `<meta charset="UTF-8">`?
options: Tells the browser which character encoding to use
explanation: UTF-8 supports all languages + emoji. Without it, accented characters break.

name: Required doctype
text: What happens if you omit DOCTYPE?
options: Browser enters 'quirks mode' and renders differently
explanation: Missing DOCTYPE triggers quirks mode — browser renders inconsistently with standards.

name: Missing close tag
text:

Content.

explanation: h1 wasn't closed.

explanation: Every img needs alt text for accessibility.

name: Mismatched quotes
text: This code has a bug. Fix it. Broken:
explanation: Open quote was double, close was single.

name: Nested close wrong order

explanation: Close tags must be in reverse order of open tags.

name: Slash in wrong spot

explanation: Unnecessary / without closing >. In HTML5 self-closing is not required.

explanation: HTML5 recommends lowercase tags consistently.

name: Basic colour
text:
What colour is the p text? Code: p { color: #ff0000; }

Hello

name: Class selector
text:
Which elements are yellow? Code: .highlight { background: yellow; }

Normal

Special
answer: Only the p with class='highlight' — the second one.

name: ID vs class
answer: The id wins because it has higher specificity (1-0-0 beats 0-1-0).

name: Cascade ordering
answer: Blue. Both rules have same specificity — last one wins.

name: Specificity id vs class
answer: Red. id (1-0-0) beats class (0-1-0) regardless of order.

name: Specificity class + element
Inherit from body
text:
What colour is the p? Code: body { color: red; }

Hi
answer: Red — color inherits down from body to p.

name: Padding inside
text:
What does padding do? Code: .box { padding: 20px; background: yellow; }
Hi
answer: Creates 20px of space INSIDE the box, between the text 'Hi' and the edge of the yellow background.

name: Margin outside
answer: Creates 20px of space OUTSIDE the yellow — pushes other elements away.

name: Border basics
answer: A rectangular box with a 2px solid blue border and 10px of internal padding.

name: Border-radius circle
answer: A perfect circle (because border-radius: 50% with equal width/height).

name: Background shorthand
answer: Dark grey background with bg.jpg image centered, not repeating.

name: Font family fallback
answer: Helvetica. If Helvetica also fails, the browser's default sans-serif font.

name: Hex shorthand
Rgba transparency
answer: Black background at 50% transparency — you can see through it.

name: Link stylesheet
text:
Retype exactly. Build CSS muscle memory.

<link rel="stylesheet" href="styles.css">

name: Type selector
Class selector
ID selector
Box model card
Universal box-sizing
Body base styles
Hover effect
Flex container
Media query mobile-first
Specificity winner
text: Which selector has HIGHEST specificity?
explanation: id (#alert) = 1-0-0. Wins every time.

name: Class prefix
text: What character starts a class selector in CSS?
explanation: Classes start with . (dot). IDs start with #.

name: Box model order
text: Order of box model layers from INSIDE out:
explanation: Content is innermost. Then padding (inside). Then border. Then margin (outside).

name: Padding vs margin
text: Which property creates space INSIDE the element?
explanation: padding = inside. margin = outside.

name: Universal rule
text: Which CSS rule do most professional websites add FIRST?
explanation: box-sizing: border-box simplifies layout math — industry standard.

name: Cascade rule
text: If two rules have EQUAL specificity, which wins?
explanation: CSS cascades top to bottom. Later declaration wins when specificity ties.

name: Colour formats
text: Which is NOT a valid CSS colour?
explanation: darkish-blue isn't a valid keyword. Valid ones: red, blue, purple, etc.

name: Pseudo-class
text: What does :hover do?
options: Applies when user's mouse is over the element
explanation: :hover applies styling when the mouse cursor is on the element.

name: External stylesheet
text: How do you link an external stylesheet to HTML?
explanation: <link rel="stylesheet" href="..."> is the correct tag.

name: Avoid IDs in CSS
text: Why do professionals avoid using #id selectors in CSS?
options: IDs have high specificity that's hard to override
IDs don't work in old browsers
explanation: Once an id selector applies, only other id or !important can override it. Specificity is 1-0-0 — very hard to beat without going nuclear.

name: Border shorthand
text: Which is valid border shorthand?
options: All are valid in any order
explanation: Border shorthand accepts values in ANY order: width, style, colour.

name: rem vs px
text: What's the advantage of rem units over px?
options: rem scales with user's root font size — more accessible
explanation: rem respects user's browser font-size preference. Users with low vision often increase default size.

name: Max-width purpose
text: Why use max-width instead of width?
options: Max-width prevents overflow on small screens
explanation: max-width sets an upper limit but allows smaller — essential for responsive layouts.

name: Line-height units
text: Best practice for line-height?
options: Use a unitless number like 1.5 or 1.6
explanation: Unitless line-height multiplies the element's font-size — scales beautifully.

name: Font stack last entry
text: The last font in a font-family stack should be...
options: A generic family keyword (sans-serif, serif, monospace)
explanation: Generic keyword ensures the browser can always fall back to something.

name: Missing semicolon
explanation: Every property-value pair ends with semicolon.

name: Missing colon
explanation: property: value — needs colon separator.

name: Wrong selector syntax
explanation: Extra dot and space before { broke the selector.

name: Semicolon after brace
explanation: No semicolon after the closing brace.

name: id with . prefix
text:
This CSS has a bug. Fix it. Broken: .title { font-size: 32px; }

Hi

Your fix:
explanation: CSS selector must match HTML — id uses # not dot.

name: margin instead of padding
explanation: padding = inside. margin = outside.

name: px with rem keyword
text:
This CSS has a bug. Fix it.

Broken:
body { font-size: 16rem; }

Your fix:
explanation: 16rem would be 256px+ — huge. rem is a multiplier.

name: Hover typo
text:
This CSS has a bug. Fix it.

Broken:
a :hover { color: red; }

Your fix:
explanation: No space between selector and pseudo-class.

name: Missing units
text:
This CSS has a bug. Fix it.

Broken:
h1 { font-size: 32; }

Your fix:
explanation: Lengths need units (px, rem, em, %, etc.).

name: Linked stylesheet wrong
text:
This CSS has a bug. Fix it.

Broken:
<link href="styles.css">

Your fix:
explanation: rel="stylesheet" is required for the browser to load it as CSS.

name: Flex basic
text:
What layout do the divs form? Code: .row { display: flex; }
A
B
C
answer: A, B, C horizontally in a row (instead of stacked vertically).

name: Justify-content center
answer: Items sit in the centre of the row with 1rem space between them.

name: Space-between
answer: First item hugs left edge, last item hugs right edge, remaining space distributed evenly between them.

name: Align-items center
answer: Items centred vertically within the flex container.

name: Flex-wrap
answer: Items wrap to the next line instead of overflowing horizontally.

name: Flex direction column
answer: Items stack vertically (but flex properties like gap still work).

name: Gap property
answer: Creates 2rem space between each flex item (no space outside the edges).

name: Flex row + gap
Centered hero
Nav flex
Card grid with wrap
Three equal columns
Main axis
text: What's the default main axis in flexbox?
explanation: Default flex-direction is row, so main axis is horizontal (left → right).

name: Space-evenly
text: What does justify-content: space-evenly do?
options: Equal space AROUND all items including edges
explanation: space-evenly puts equal gaps at both ends + between items. Different from space-around.

name: Flex: 1
text: What does flex: 1 do to an item?
options: Makes it grow to fill available space
explanation: flex: 1 is shorthand for flex: 1 1 0% — grow to fill, shrink if needed, start at 0 basis.

name: Justify vs align
text: justify-content works on which axis?
explanation: justify-content = main axis. align-items = cross axis.

name: Gap vs margin
text: Why use gap instead of margins?
options: Gap only adds space BETWEEN items, no extra at edges
explanation: Gap is purpose-built for flex/grid — cleaner than margin hacks.

name: Readable line length
text: Optimal line length for body text?
explanation: 60-75 chars per line is the readability sweet spot. Use max-width: 65ch.

name: Body font size min
text: Minimum body font size on web?
explanation: 16px is the minimum comfortable body size. 18-20px often better on modern screens.

name: Line-height body
text: Best line-height for body text?
explanation: 1.5-1.7 for body text. 1.2-1.3 for headings.

name: Font stack required
text: Why always add fallbacks to font-family?
options: If the preferred font fails to load, fallback still works
explanation: If Inter fails, browser tries next font. End with a generic keyword (sans-serif, serif).

name: Google Fonts
text: What service hosts free web fonts with built-in @font-face?
explanation: Google Fonts is the most widely-used free web font service.

name: WCAG AA contrast body
text: Minimum contrast ratio for BODY text at WCAG AA?
explanation: 4.5:1 for body text. 3:1 for large text (18pt+). 7:1 is AAA (stricter).

name: POUR letters
text: What does POUR stand for?
explanation: POUR is the WCAG accessibility mnemonic: 4 principles of accessible web.

name: Hex #ff0000
text: What colour is #ff0000?
explanation: #ff0000 = pure red. FF red, 00 green, 00 blue.

name: Hex #000000
text: What colour is #000000?
explanation: All zeros = no colour = black.

name: Hex shorthand
text: What does #f60 expand to?
explanation: Hex shorthand: each letter doubles. f→ff, 6→66, 0→00 = #ff6600 (orange).

name: rgba alpha
text: In rgba(255, 0, 0, 0.5), what does 0.5 mean?
explanation: Alpha channel. 0 = fully transparent, 1 = opaque.

name: Typeface vs font
text: Difference between typeface and font?
options: Typeface = the design (Helvetica). Font = the file that delivers it.
explanation: Typeface is the artistic design. Font is the implementation (file).

name: Your font pairing
text: What font pairing will you use for your portfolio? (1 heading + 1 body font). Why? 4 sentences.

name: WCAG contrast fail
text: Describe a colour pair that WOULD fail WCAG AA (4.5:1). Then describe one that passes. Include hex codes.

name: POUR in your own words
text: Explain POUR (Perceivable, Operable, Understandable, Robust) in your own words. Give one concrete example per letter.

name: Cite/q/blockquote
text: Find a real quote online. Write the HTML to mark it up semantically using <blockquote> and <cite>. Paste the HTML.

name: Mobile first
answer: Red. The media query requires 768px+ to activate.

name: Tablet breakpoint
text:
Which background on a 800px tablet?

Code:
Same code as above
answer: Blue. 800px > 768px, so the media query activates.

name: Viewport meta
text:
What does the viewport meta tag do?

Code:
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
answer: Tells phone browsers to use their actual screen width (not pretend to be desktop).

name: Image responsive
answer: Image scales down if container is small, maintains aspect ratio.

name: Clamp for fluid type
answer: Font size scales fluidly between 1.5rem and 3rem based on viewport width (5vw).

name: Viewport meta
text:
Retype exactly:

<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">

name: Mobile-first media query
Image responsive
Fluid type clamp
text:
Retype exactly:

h1 { font-size: clamp(1.5rem, 5vw, 3rem); }

name: 3 breakpoint layout
text:
Design a 3-breakpoint responsive CSS: 1 col on phone (<768), 2 on tablet (768-1024), 3 on desktop (1024+). Use CSS Grid.

Sketch out the CSS. Paste here:

name: Mobile nav
text:
Write CSS to make a nav horizontal on desktop, stacked vertically on mobile (<768px).

Your CSS:

name: Fluid hero
text:
Write CSS for a hero section that: is 400px tall on mobile, 600px tall on desktop (768+), with fluid h1 font-size using clamp.

Your CSS:

name: Hide on mobile
text:
Write CSS to hide an element on phones (<768px) and show it on tablet+.

Your CSS:

name: Card-to-list on mobile
text:
On desktop: cards in a 3-col grid. On mobile: stacked full-width list. Write the CSS.

Your CSS:

name: Image with breakpoints
text:
Write HTML + CSS for an image that's 100px on mobile, 200px on tablet, 300px on desktop.

Your code:

name: Typography scale
text:
Write a type scale using clamp for h1, h2, h3 that scales fluidly between mobile and desktop.

Your CSS:

name: Container max-width
text:
Write CSS for a centred container with max-width 1200px + horizontal auto margins + 1rem horizontal padding.

Your CSS:

name: Create index.html
text: Create a file called index.html with HTML5 boilerplate. Save it to your portfolio folder. Preview in Live Server.
hint: Do this in VS Code. Come back to the arcade to mark complete.
flavour: Real VS Code work. Mark complete when done.

name: Link stylesheet
text: Create styles.css in the same folder. Link it in your index.html head. Preview.
hint: Do this in VS Code. Come back to the arcade to mark complete.
flavour: Real VS Code work. Mark complete when done.

name: Add your hero
text: Build a hero section in index.html: h1 with your name, p with tagline, a button with 'Learn more'. Style it.
hint: Do this in VS Code. Come back to the arcade to mark complete.
flavour: Real VS Code work. Mark complete when done.

name: About section
text: Add an <section id='about'> with 2-3 paragraphs about you + your profile photo.
hint: Do this in VS Code. Come back to the arcade to mark complete.
flavour: Real VS Code work. Mark complete when done.

name: Nav structure
text: Build a <nav> inside <header> with links to #about, #projects, #contact. Make nav sticky.
hint: Do this in VS Code. Come back to the arcade to mark complete.
flavour: Real VS Code work. Mark complete when done.

name: 3 project cards
text: Create 3 .project-card divs with image + title + description. Use CSS Grid to lay them out.
hint: Do this in VS Code. Come back to the arcade to mark complete.
flavour: Real VS Code work. Mark complete when done.

name: Footer
text: Add a <footer> with your name, year, copyright symbol (©). Style it with dark background.
hint: Do this in VS Code. Come back to the arcade to mark complete.
flavour: Real VS Code work. Mark complete when done.

name: Contact form
text: Add a <form> with name, email, message inputs + submit button. Use Formspree URL as action.
hint: Do this in VS Code. Come back to the arcade to mark complete.
flavour: Real VS Code work. Mark complete when done.

name: Mobile responsive
text: Add mobile-first media queries for 768px + 1024px breakpoints. Test at DevTools 320/768/1440.
hint: Do this in VS Code. Come back to the arcade to mark complete.
flavour: Real VS Code work. Mark complete when done.

name: Add transitions
text: Add smooth hover transitions to your nav links, buttons, and project cards.
hint: Do this in VS Code. Come back to the arcade to mark complete.
flavour: Real VS Code work. Mark complete when done.

name: Google Fonts
text: Import a Google Font in your head. Apply it to your body font-family.
hint: Do this in VS Code. Come back to the arcade to mark complete.
flavour: Real VS Code work. Mark complete when done.

name: Colour palette
text: Choose 4 hex codes. Add them as CSS variables in :root. Apply throughout your site.
hint: Do this in VS Code. Come back to the arcade to mark complete.
flavour: Real VS Code work. Mark complete when done.

name: Meta description
text: Add a <meta name='description'> to your head with 160 char description of your portfolio.
hint: Do this in VS Code. Come back to the arcade to mark complete.
flavour: Real VS Code work. Mark complete when done.

name: Favicon
text: Create or find a favicon.ico (or .png). Add it to your head.
hint: Do this in VS Code. Come back to the arcade to mark complete.
flavour: Real VS Code work. Mark complete when done.

name: Optimize images
text: Run all your images through Squoosh.app. Aim for each under 200KB.
hint: Do this in VS Code. Come back to the arcade to mark complete.
flavour: Real VS Code work. Mark complete when done.

name: Lighthouse audit
text: Run Chrome Lighthouse on your portfolio. Screenshot the scores.
hint: Do this in VS Code. Come back to the arcade to mark complete.
flavour: Real VS Code work. Mark complete when done.

name: W3C validate
text: Paste your HTML at validator.w3.org. Fix any errors.
hint: Do this in VS Code. Come back to the arcade to mark complete.
flavour: Real VS Code work. Mark complete when done.

name: WAVE audit
text: Install WAVE extension. Run on your portfolio. Fix any errors.
hint: Do this in VS Code. Come back to the arcade to mark complete.
flavour: Real VS Code work. Mark complete when done.

name: Alt text review
text: Check every . Add or improve alt text. None should be empty unless decorative.
hint: Do this in VS Code. Come back to the arcade to mark complete.
flavour: Real VS Code work. Mark complete when done.

name: Publish GitHub Pages
text: Create GitHub repo, push your files, enable Pages in Settings. Share URL.
hint: Do this in VS Code. Come back to the arcade to mark complete.
flavour: Real VS Code work. Mark complete when done.

name: Audience statement
text: Who is YOUR portfolio for? Use the template: 'For [audience] who [need], my portfolio [offers what] so they can [goal].'

name: Design decision
text: Describe ONE design decision you made (colour, font, layout). Why that choice? What principle does it apply?

name: Hardest thing
text: Name the ONE hardest thing about your portfolio build so far. How did you overcome it (or are overcoming it)?

name: What I'd do differently
text: If you could restart the portfolio from zero, what would you do differently? 4 sentences.

name: Accessibility check
text: Name 3 specific accessibility fixes you made after your Lighthouse audit. Include the issue and your fix.

name: Career pathway
text: Which career pathway (front-end dev, UX designer, accessibility specialist, web content manager) interests you? Why?

name: Lighthouse tabs
text: Which Lighthouse category covers colour contrast + alt text?
explanation: Accessibility tab checks WCAG compliance including contrast and alt.

name: W3C vs Lighthouse
text: What does W3C validator catch that Lighthouse doesn't?
explanation: W3C is a syntax validator. Lighthouse checks quality signals but not strict spec compliance.

name: Keyboard nav test
text: How do you test if your site is keyboard-accessible?
options: Use only Tab key to navigate through focusable elements
explanation: Tab through the site with no mouse. Can you reach + activate everything?

name: Alt text empty vs missing
text: When is alt='' (empty) correct?
options: When the image is purely decorative
explanation: Decorative images get empty alt so screen readers skip them. Meaningful images need descriptive alt.

name: Boss: First Page Published
text: Build a complete single-page HTML document (DOCTYPE, html, head, title, meta, body, h1, p, at least one ul, at least one img with alt). Paste your HTML here.
flavour: This is a mini capstone. Show you can build a complete HTML page from memory.

name: Boss: Semantic Homepage
text: Build the HTML skeleton for a 'homepage' using ONLY semantic elements (no divs). Include: header with nav, main with 2 sections and 1 article, aside, footer. Use valid structure.
flavour: Prove you understand when to use which semantic element.

name: Boss: Styled Card Component
text: Write HTML + CSS for a .project-card component. It must include: image, title (h3), description, link. Style it with box model (padding, border, border-radius), typography, colours. Use box-sizing: border-box. Make it hoverable.
flavour: One complete, reusable component. Paste both HTML and CSS.

name: Boss: Flexbox Layout
text: Build an HTML + CSS page with: horizontal nav (flex), 3-card row (flex with wrap), centred hero section (flex column centred). All responsive-friendly.

name: Boss: Accessible Colour + Type System
text: Design a complete type + colour system for your portfolio. Deliverables: 2 font-families with fallbacks, type scale (h1/h2/h3/body sizes + line-heights), 4-colour palette with hex codes + WCAG AA ratios listed for every text/background pair.

name: Boss: Responsive Portfolio Homepage
text: Build a complete homepage (Alex's OR your own) with: responsive hero, sticky nav, about section, 3-card project grid, footer. Mobile-first. Test at 320/768/1440px.

name: Boss: Own Portfolio Complete
text: Ship all pages of YOUR portfolio (home + about + projects + contact). Apply everything learned. Paste the structure of your HTML files + confirm they preview locally.

name: Boss: Published
text: Publish your portfolio to GitHub Pages (or Netlify Drop). Paste your LIVE URL + your Lighthouse accessibility score (screenshot description).
flavour: Live on the internet. The ultimate boss.

name: Boss: Peer Presentation
text: Present your portfolio URL to the class for 2 minutes. Answer 2 peer questions about design decisions. Paste a summary of the feedback you received (Two Stars + a Wish).

name: Boss: Evaluation + Goal
text: Complete your 1-page written evaluation (statement of intent, design decisions, accessibility, iteration, final evaluation, AI log). Set one SMART goal for Term 3. Paste the goal here.
